title: Mid-render compute for graphics processing
abstract: Techniques are disclosed relating to performing mid-render auxiliary compute tasks for graphics processing. In some embodiments, auxiliary compute tasks are performed during a render pass, using at least a portion of a memory context of the render pass, without accessing a shared memory during the render pass. Relative to flushing render data to shared memory to perform compute tasks, this may reduce memory accesses and/or cache thrashing, which may in turn increase performance and/or reduce power consumption.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An apparatus, comprising: a plurality of shader processing elements, including first and second shader processing elements, wherein the apparatus is configured to implement a local memory space that is accessible to the first shader processing element but not the second shader processing element; a shared memory that is accessible to both the first and second shader processing elements; wherein the first shader processing element is configured to: perform a render pass, including by accessing graphics data from the shared memory; during the render pass, perform an auxiliary task to generate and store data to the local memory space; and continue the render pass, including by accessing the data stored in the local memory space by the auxiliary task. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the auxiliary task includes generating a light list. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the auxiliary task includes processing based on a reduction algorithm. 4. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the auxiliary task accesses data in the local memory space that is generated by the render pass. 5. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the shared memory is a cache and the render pass does not write to the cache until completion of operations in the render pass. 6. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising: one or more storage elements configured to store dimensions of a data structure in the local memory space, wherein the dimensions are configurable by a graphics program that includes the render pass; wherein the auxiliary task is configured to access the one or more storage elements to determine dimensions of the data structure and access the data structure based on the determined dimensions. 7. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium having stored thereon design information that specifies a design of at least a portion of a hardware integrated circuit in a format recognized by a semiconductor fabrication system that is configured to use the design information to produce the circuit according to the design, including: a plurality of shader processing elements, including first and second shader processing elements, wherein the circuit is configured to implement a local memory space that is accessible to the first shader processing element but not the second shader processing element; a shared memory that is accessible to both the first and second shader processing elements; wherein the first shader processing element is configured to: perform a render pass, including by accessing graphics data from the shared memory; during the render pass, perform an auxiliary task to generate and store data to the local memory space; and continue the render pass, including by accessing the data stored in the local memory space by the auxiliary task, wherein the render pass does not write data to the shared memory prior to the auxiliary task. 8. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 7, wherein the auxiliary task includes generating a light list. 9. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 7, wherein the auxiliary task includes generating a plurality of mipmap levels. 10. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 7, wherein the render pass includes a plurality of fragment processing operations and wherein the auxiliary task does not include fragment processing operations. 1 1. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 7, wherein the shared memory is a first level cache shared by the plurality of shade processing elements. 12. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 7, wherein the design information further specifies that the circuit includes: 

 one or more storage elements configured to store dimensions of a data structure in the local memory space, wherein the dimensions are configurable by a graphics program that includes the render pass; wherein the auxiliary task is configured to access the one or more storage elements to determine dimensions of the data structure and access the data structure based on the determined dimensions. 13. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 7, wherein the local memory space is implemented using one or more on-chip storage elements. 14. An apparatus, comprising: a plurality of shader processing elements, including first and second shader processing elements, wherein the apparatus is configured to implement a local memory space that is accessible to the first shader processing element but not the second shader processing element; a shared memory that is accessible to both the first and second shader processing elements; wherein the first shader processing element is configured to: perform a first portion of a set of operations, including by accessing graphics data from the shared memory and processing the graphics data using the local memory space; perform an auxiliary task to generate compute data and store the compute data to the local memory space; perform a second portion of the set of operations, including by accessing the compute data stored in the local memory space by the auxiliary task; and write results of the set of operations to the shared memory; and wherein the first shader processing element does not write to the shared memory between beginning to perform the first portion of the set of operations and writing the results to the shared memory such that the set of operations and the auxiliary task appear to be performed atomically to the second shader processing element. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the auxiliary task includes generating a light list and storing the light list in the local memory space. 16. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the auxiliary task includes generating data for a plurality of mipmap levels from a texture and storing the mipmap levels in the local memory space. 17. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the operations in the set of operations are pixel shading operations and wherein the auxiliary task does not include pixel shading operations. 18. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the set of operations corresponds to a render pass. 19. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the shared memory is a cache. 20. The apparatus of claim 14, further comprising: one or more storage elements configured to store dimensions of a data structure in the local memory space, wherein the dimensions are configurable by a graphics program that includes the set of operations; wherein the auxiliary task is configured to read the one or more storage elements to determine dimensions of the data structure.
